verbatim_quote: And now there were reports that this madman who called himself Euron Crow's Eye was even sending longships up Whispering Sound toward Oldtown.
note: Direct confirmation of Euron's ironborn raiding toward Oldtown

verbatim_quote: What feeds a dragon's fire? All Valyrian sorcery was rooted in blood or fire. The sorcerers of the Freehold could see across mountains, seas, and deserts with one of these glass candles.
note: Confirms glass candles as magical Valyrian artifacts housed in Citadel

verbatim_quote: And of course there was even less chance of his coming on the fragmentary, anonymous, blood-soaked tome sometimes called Blood and Fire and sometimes The Death of Dragons, the only surviving copy of which was supposedly hidden away in a locked vault beneath the Citadel.
note: Confirms existence of 'Death of Dragons' tome at Citadel; Faceless Man infiltration is book knowledge

verbatim_quote: A thousand years old, that was. We found it in a giant's grave, and no man o' us had ever seen a horn so big. That must have been why Mance got the notion to tell you it were Joramun's. He wanted you crows to think he had it in his power to blow your bloody Wall down about your knees. But we never found the true horn, not for all our digging.
note: Confirms Mance's horn was fake and that the true Horn of Winter was never found

verbatim_quote: The warhorn he had given to Sam. On closer examination the horn had proved cracked, and even after he had cleaned all the dirt out, Jon had been unable to get any sound from it.
note: Confirms Jon found a horn and gave it to Sam; both thought it worthless

The hits provided (agot-bran-03.md:93 and adwd-theon-01.md:255) both refer to Bran's or other characters' dreams of 'the heart of winter', not Euron's childhood dream. Beat claims Euron saw this in his own flying dream, which is not grounded in published text (Forsaken chapter is unreleased)

verbatim_quote: Call it the Horn of Darkness. If the Wall falls, night falls as well, the long night that never ends.
note: Confirms lore that White Walkers bring an endless Long Night

verbatim_quote: IRONMEN, said Euron Greyjoy, you have heard my horn. Now hear my words.
note: Confirms Euron sounds his horn at kingsmoot; does not specify three times but is the primary Euron horn sounding at kingsmoot. Three-horn-blast parallel to Night's Watch signal is speculation

verbatim_quote: The last of her line. They say she is the fairest woman in the world. Her hair is silver-gold, and her eyes are amethysts . . . but you need not take my word for it, brother.
note: Euron directly tells Victarion that Daenerys has amethyst eyes; symbolic linguistic link to Bloodstone Emperor's sister confirmed

verbatim_quote: Protect me from the Silence. He laughed. Godless? Why, Aeron, I am the godliest man ever to raise sail!
note: Euron references his ship the Silence and establishes his power through fear and manipulation; 'blood eye' is consistently used as his hidden eye throughout

verbatim_quote: I swore to give you Westeros, and here is your first taste. A morsel, nothing more . . . but we shall feast before the fall of night!
note: Euron's promises of feasting and conquest; Silence ship = opposite of Song; his power based on silence and fear

Exact quote 'Those who follow me shall feast until the end of their days' not found; closest match is 'Those who follow me will feast until the end of their days' in affc-the-drowned-man-01.md but beat attributes it as title-thematic framing of A Feast for Crows itself

verbatim_quote: Euron had seduced them with his glib tongue and smiling eye and bound them to his cause with the plunder of half a hundred distant lands
note: Confirms Euron's power comes from manipulation, not martial prowess; he wins kingsmoot through speech and bribery, not violence

verbatim_quote: I threw it in the sea during one of my dark moods.
note: Euron admits throwing a dragon egg into the sea, but provides no explanation for how he came by it; beat's skepticism about his claim is warranted
